title: Domain Role Definition Schema V1
description: JSON Schema for domain-scoped role definitions within a domain-physics document. Domain roles define a hierarchy beneath the Domain Authority ceiling, allowing each domain to create its own access tiers (e.g., teacher/teaching-assistant/student in education, site-manager/field-operator/observer in agriculture).

role_id.description: Domain-scoped role identifier. Unique within the domain-physics document. lowercase_snake_case, 2-48 characters. Must not collide with system role IDs (root, domain_authority, it_support, qa, auditor, user, guest).

role_name.description: Human-readable display name for this domain role.

hierarchy_level.description: Position in the domain role hierarchy. 1 = highest privilege (just below Domain Authority), 10 = lowest. The Domain Authority is implicitly level 0 and must not be declared in the roles array.

description.description: Purpose and responsibilities of this domain role.

maps_to_system_role.description: The system-level role this domain role maps to for permission ceiling enforcement. Determines the maximum possible permissions this domain role can be granted. Cross-cutting system roles (root, it_support, qa, auditor) cannot be used here.

default_access.description: Default permission characters for this role within the domain module. Must not exceed the access that maps_to_system_role would grant.

may_assign_domain_roles.description: Whether holders of this role can assign domain roles to other users within this domain. Only the Domain Authority and roles with this flag set to true may assign roles.

max_assignable_level.description: The highest hierarchy_level (numerically, i.e., lowest privilege) this role can assign. A role can only assign roles at this level or higher (lower privilege). Only meaningful when may_assign_domain_roles is true.

scoped_capabilities.description: Domain-specific capability flags. Free-form boolean map consulted by domain runtime code for fine-grained capability checks beyond the rwxi permission model (e.g., receive_escalations, view_student_progress, issue_hints).
